content: The spelling variant 'reflexion' (the less common British English spelling of reflection used in high school vocabulary curricula) can be framed as a natural 'self-contrast' test case for LLM self-reflection mechanisms. LLMs trained primarily on modern American English will often output the more common 'reflection' when asked to proofread text using 'reflexion', creating a low-stakes, accessible test of an LLM's ability to reflect on inconsistent outputs across different linguistic conventions.
